beats.title: Breakfast in the Hold
beats.boundaryReason: Opening recap and the first ruined-barracks search form one continuous scene.
dateEvidence: The session manifest starts on 1748-04-13 and the party is finishing breakfast.

beats.title: Loot and Gnoll Clues
beats.boundaryReason: The party continues searching the outbuildings, uncovers treasure, and gathers clues about the gnolls.
dateEvidence: This still follows directly from breakfast with no rest or travel break.

beats.title: Shadow Warnings and Supplies
beats.boundaryReason: The party regroups, sorts loot, discusses Delwath's shadow-message clues, and decides how to proceed.
dateEvidence: No day transition appears before the party moves toward the mists.

beats.title: Testing the Courtyard Mists
beats.boundaryReason: The focus shifts from looting to direct investigation of the tainted water, mists, and summoning circle.
dateEvidence: The DM says it is still morning but probably just before lunchtime as they approach the bridge.

beats.title: Bridge Circle and Battle
beats.boundaryReason: Destroying the circle immediately triggers the demonic plant battle, which stays one continuous combat scene.
dateEvidence: The bridge investigation continues without a break and the DM places the scene near noon.

beats.title: Scroll Ash and Animals
beats.boundaryReason: After the battle, the party studies the cleared corruption, finds the scroll-cap remains, and frees the trapped animals.
dateEvidence: This aftermath follows immediately after the fight on the same day.

beats.title: Outpost Guardian and Forge
beats.boundaryReason: The party leaves the valley and explores the dwarven outpost, including the stone guardian and ruined mineworks.
dateEvidence: The published session note places the outpost visit in the afternoon after the noon battle.

beats.title: Camp on the South Road
beats.boundaryReason: The outpost exploration ends and the session closes with the ride south and evening camp.
dateEvidence: The DM says they ride south for a few hours and make camp for the evening.
